mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in > 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that compresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive returns occur in > 50% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production trigger immediate market repricing of supply chain disruptions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during early-cycle contraction phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during contraction regimes or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) in contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, monetary policy reaches the effective lower bound, eliminating further rate-cut catalysts and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to liquidity trap dynamics.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total employment and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market breakdown, which suppresses earnings growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive returns dominate (> 55%) when the divergence widens.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect stronger aggregate demand that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansions in industrial production signal resolving supply bottlenecks that improve inventory turnover and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery regimes or 5-day forward returns lack statistical significance (p > 0.05) in recovery periods.
